text: Does coupled Burgers-swept-KdV decompose a Gaussian wave packet (amp=4, sigma=1.5) in v into a soliton train by T=6.0 (>=2 well-separated peaks each >=0.8, mass drift <8%)?

method: Fourier pseudospectral (no dealiasing), classical explicit RK4 on full RHS including v_xxx (baseline per progressive-complexity discipline)
bank_use_rationale: Per progressive-complexity discipline, E1 must be the simplest baseline; positive entries confirm spectral discretization is appropriate for tracking soliton amplitude/phase in this PDE class (kb-kdv-spectral-solitonAmplitude-conservation), and the amplitude threshold (kb-kdv-smallAmplitude-dispersiveRegime) reassures that amp=4 is well above the dispersive-only regime. Stronger entries (kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass, kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation, BKdV-S1 deep-synthesis) suggest dealiasing+IMEX-CN as ultimate escalation target, but those are reserved for E2/E3 if the clean baseline fails. dt=1e-4 < textbook dispersion-CFL ~1.47e-4 for raw band.

rationale: Classic aliasing failure: at amp=4 the quadratic products (v^2, u*v, v*v_x) feed energy into modes near k_Nyquist that alias back into the resolved band, producing exponential blow-up by t~0.28 with overflow in v*v. dt=1e-4 was within dispersion-CFL for the raw band so this is aliasing, not dispersive stiffness. Matches the failure mode that motivated the 2/3-rule cutoff in BKdV-S1.

Single-component upgrade per progressive-complexity discipline: keep Fourier pseudospectral + classical RK4, dt=1e-4, but add 2/3-rule dealiasing (zero modes with |k_idx|>Nx/3) on every nonlinear product. This is precisely the upgrade BKdV-S1 deep-synthesis entries validate for the same PDE class.

ic: v0=4*exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), u0=0; L=30, Nx=256 (projected onto 2/3 band)
method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product (v^2, u*v, u*u_x, v*v_x) + classical explicit RK4 on full RHS (incl. v_xxx). Single-component upgrade over E1.
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S1 deep-synthesis (depth>1) entries are the strongest positive signal: identical PDE class (coupled Burgers-swept-KdV), identical Nx=256 / L=30 / dt=2e-4 stack, demonstrated to reach T=10 cleanly at amp up to 3 with 2/3-rule + RK4. I adopt the exact dealiasing recipe and use dt=1e-4 (tighter than their 2e-4) because amp=4 is slightly above the validated range, so I want extra margin on the post-dealias CFL bound (~4.94e-4). kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation reinforces that dealiasing is essential at this amplitude. kb-kdv-spectral-solitonAmplitude-conservation confirms spectral methods preserve mass/amplitude well enough for soliton-train measurement. I do NOT switch to IMEX-CN yet (would be a 2-component upgrade); that is reserved for E3 if E2 fails on dispersive stiffness rather than aliasing.

rationale: Phenomenon target met decisively. (a) 10 distinct peaks in final v all exceed 0.8 (target was >=2 each >=0.8); minimum separation 1.52 in x is well above any sensible 'well-separated' threshold. (b) mass(v) drift is essentially zero (~3e-16 relative, vastly below the 8% tolerance). (c) Initial Gaussian (single peak at x=-5 with amp=4) has visibly decomposed into a soliton-train-like array spanning the periodic domain - a clean signature of KdV-type inverse-scattering decomposition. (d) sup_u peaked around 4.9 with oscillations (consistent with BKdV-S1 amp=3 observations rescaled), no overflow, no aliasing into top band. Saved snapshots at 13 evenly-spaced times for the conservation check.

Stop early per protocol: F2.useful_self_assessment=true with phenomenon target solidly met (10 peaks vs >=2 required, mass drift <1e-15 vs <8% required). E2 is the final answer; candidate.py and pred_results/T_B.npy correspond to this run.
